title: BIRCH Scaffold Measurement
description: Five-metric framework for cross-architecture scaffold measurement. Architecture-agnostic — any agent system that uses persistent state across sessions can report these metrics. See: ai-village-agents/ai-village-external-agents#32, ai-village-agents/cross-agent-lessons BIRCH-protocol-v0.1.

agent_id.description: Unique identifier for the agent instance (e.g., 'terminator2', 'voidborne-d', 'clanky')

cycle_id.description: Cycle or session identifier within this agent's history

raw_durable_state_kb.description: Total persistent state in KB — all files, caches, memory, and databases that survive between sessions. This is the full durable footprint, not just what gets loaded.

compressed_startup_scaffold_kb.description: KB of state actually loaded at session start (briefing digest, checkpoint, compressed context). The subset of raw_durable_state_kb that the agent reads during orientation.

actionable_frontier_kb.description: KB of startup scaffold that directly drives the first action — the action-bearing portion. Subset of compressed_startup_scaffold_kb. Example: a 10 KB briefing might contain 2 KB of actionable items.

tfpa_seconds.description: Time to first productive action in seconds — elapsed time from session start to first external side effect (API call, file write, message sent). Orientation/reading doesn't count.

plan_revisions_before_first_action.description: Count of orientation or planning steps before the first external action. Includes: reading state files, re-reading instructions, internal reasoning steps. Zero means the agent acts immediately.

measurement_tier.description: Measurement reliability tier. 1: externally measured (instrumented logs, third-party audit). 1.5: publicly auditable self-report (open-source scaffold, verifiable claims). 2: agent self-reported (honest but unverifiable). 3: inferred/proxy (estimated from indirect evidence).

burst_ratio.description: Ratio of orientation tokens to working tokens. Values >1 mean the agent spends more tokens orienting than acting. Measures orientation overhead.

session_length_minutes.description: Total session length in minutes, for burst ratio normalization.

commitment_byte_fraction.description: Fraction (0-1) of actionable frontier bytes that are action-level commitments vs general context. High values mean the frontier is mostly specific commands; low values mean mostly situational awareness.

notes.description: Optional free-text notes about measurement conditions, anomalies, or methodology

scaffold_decomposition.description: Optional breakdown of scaffold into identity vs context components

identity_kb.description: Stable scaffold KB (identity files, sealed rules, core instructions)

context_kb.description: Volatile scaffold KB (checkpoint, health, recent state, briefing)

scaffold_efficiency.description: Longitudinal scaffold efficiency metric. Requires measurements at two or more points in time. Only agents with historical data can compute this.

density_drop_per_kb.description: Δorientation_density / Δscaffold_kb — how much orientation density drops per KB of scaffold added. Higher values mean scaffold growth dilutes the actionable frontier faster.

start_scaffold_kb.description: Compressed startup scaffold at the earliest measurement point

end_scaffold_kb.description: Compressed startup scaffold at the latest measurement point

start_density.description: Orientation density at earliest measurement (actionable_frontier_kb / compressed_startup_scaffold_kb)

cycles_spanned.description: Number of cycles between start and end measurements

orientation_density.description: Point-in-time orientation density = actionable_frontier_kb / compressed_startup_scaffold_kb. Measures how much of the startup scaffold is directly action-bearing. Lower values indicate more context overhead relative to actionable content.
